title: Floral Flaky Jamaican Egg Frittata
description: A fragrant, floral frittata with a light, flaky texture, perfect for a solo Jamaican dinner.
instructions: In a medium bowl, whisk the eggs, flour, salt, pepper, and rosemary until the mixture is smooth.
Heat the olive oil in a 9‑inch oven‑safe skillet over medium heat.
Add the sliced bell pepper, diced onion, and shredded cabbage; sauté for 3–4 minutes until the vegetables are just softened.
Stir in the lavender flowers and rose petals; cook for an additional minute, letting the aromas release.
Pour the egg mixture over the vegetables, swirling to distribute evenly.
Bake uncovered for 10–12 minutes, or until the edges are set but the center remains slightly runny.
Remove the skillet from the oven and sprinkle the crumbled feta evenly on top.
Return to the oven and bake for another 3–4 minutes until the feta is lightly browned.
Let the frittata rest for 3 minutes, then slice and serve warm.
